title: Abbott i-STAT 1 Blood Gas Analyzer - Cartridge Not Recognized or Insertion Errors
description: Troubleshooting cartridge recognition and insertion errors caused by seating, damage, or contact issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while actively testing a patient sample. Use a backup analyzer if immediate testing is required.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Insert a cartridge and observe for 'Cartridge not recognized', no response, or repeated prompts to confirm the issue is reproducible.

steps.title: Check Cartridge Compatibility and Expiration (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Confirm correct cartridge type, check expiration date, and verify proper storage conditions.

steps.title: Inspect Cartridge for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check for cracks, bent contacts, debris, or fluid contamination on cartridge contacts.

steps.title: Verify Proper Cartridge Insertion Technique
steps.instructions: Ensure cartridge is fully seated, properly aligned, and inserted without angling.

steps.title: Inspect Analyzer Cartridge Port (High-Value Check)
steps.instructions: Check for debris, contamination, or damaged pins inside the cartridge port and clean if needed.

steps.title: Perform Analyzer Restart
steps.instructions: Power off, wait 10–15 seconds, then power on and retest.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Cartridge
steps.instructions: Use a cartridge from a different lot or verified stock to rule out batch issues.

CCR.Complaint: Analyzer not recognizing cartridges during blood gas testing.
CCR.Cause: Expired and improperly stored cartridges failed to establish contact.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with in-date cartridges stored per guidelines; analyzer function restored.

helpfulDetails: Alarm behavior or error messages observed

title: Abbott i-STAT 1 Blood Gas Analyzer - Analyzer Lockout or QC Failures
description: Troubleshooting analyzer lockout or QC failures on the Abbott i-STAT 1 due to quality control, cartridge, or configuration-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the analyzer is needed for active patient testing. Move testing to a backup analyzer if available.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Lockout or QC Failure
steps.instructions: Power on the analyzer and review the display. Confirm lockout message or QC failure notification, note any specific error messages or codes, and attempt to access testing mode.

steps.title: Check Operator ID and Authorization Settings (Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Verify operator ID is entered correctly, confirm operator certification status if required, and try a known authorized operator ID.

steps.title: Verify Quality Control (QC) Requirements
steps.instructions: Check QC status in the analyzer menu, confirm QC has been performed within the required timeframe, and review the last QC result.

steps.title: Perform Required QC Test
steps.instructions: Use appropriate QC material, ensure cartridges are within expiration and stored properly, and run QC per facility protocol.

steps.title: Inspect Test Cartridges (Frequent Contributor to QC Failure)
steps.instructions: Check expiration dates, confirm proper storage conditions, allow cartridges to reach room temperature, and try a new cartridge from a different lot.

steps.title: Check Analyzer Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure proper operating temperature, avoid drafts or sunlight, and allow the device to acclimate if recently moved.

steps.title: Inspect Analyzer Contacts and Cartridge Port
steps.instructions: Inspect the cartridge port for debris, ensure contacts are clean and dry, and avoid inserting damaged cartridges.

steps.title: Perform Analyzer Reset
steps.instructions: Power off the analyzer, remove batteries if applicable, restart, and reattempt QC.

CCR.Complaint: Analyzer locked out and unable to run patient tests due to QC failure.
CCR.Cause: Expired QC requirement and failed QC due to outdated cartridge lot.
CCR.Resolution: Performed QC with new cartridge, passed QC, and restored analyzer to service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or removed)

title: Abbott i-STAT 1 Blood Gas Analyzer - Wireless Download or Connectivity Failure
description: Troubleshooting wireless transmission failures, connectivity issues, and docking-related download problems on the Abbott i-STAT 1 analyzer.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the analyzer is actively being used for patient testing. Complete testing or switch to another device first.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Connectivity Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to transmit results or connect to the system. Confirm presence or absence of wireless connection indicators and check for failed transmission messages or missing results in LIS/EMR.

steps.title: Check Wireless Signal Availability (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Move the device closer to a known access point or docking station and check signal strength indicator if available.

steps.title: Verify Analyzer Wireless Settings
steps.instructions: Access device settings/menu, confirm wireless is enabled, and verify correct network profile is selected.

steps.title: Check Docking Station or Base Unit (If Used)
steps.instructions: Ensure dock has power, confirm network cable is connected if applicable, and reseat analyzer in dock.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical or Environmental Interference
steps.instructions: Look for nearby equipment causing interference, avoid metal cabinets or shielded rooms, and confirm no visible antenna damage.

steps.title: Restart the Analyzer
steps.instructions: Turn the device off and back on, then attempt transmission again.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Network or Dock
steps.instructions: Try connecting in a different known working area or dock and compare with another i-STAT device.

CCR.Complaint: Analyzer not transmitting results wirelessly to the system.
CCR.Cause: Weak wireless signal and improper docking station connection.
CCR.Resolution: Moved device to proper coverage area, reseated in powered dock, and verified successful transmission.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (operational or removed from service)

title: Abbott i-STAT 1 Blood Gas Analyzer - Battery Not Charging or Shortened Runtime
description: Troubleshooting battery charging issues, short runtime, and unexpected shutdowns on the Abbott i-STAT 1 analyzer.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the analyzer is actively being used for patient testing. Move testing to a backup device.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to power the unit and place it in the charger/docking station. Observe for no charging indicator, rapid battery drain, or unexpected shutdown.

steps.title: Check Charging Station Power (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Confirm outlet is working, check power adapter connection, and verify dock indicator lights.

steps.title: Inspect Analyzer Seating in Dock
steps.instructions: Reseat the analyzer, ensure correct orientation, and check for obstructions preventing contact.

steps.title: Inspect Charging Contacts (Common Issue)
steps.instructions: Check for dirt or corrosion, clean contacts, and verify they are not damaged.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Charger or Dock
steps.instructions: Place the analyzer in a confirmed working dock to isolate whether the issue is with the dock or analyzer.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Performance
steps.instructions: Fully charge the unit, remove it, and monitor runtime to assess battery condition.

steps.title: Perform Power Cycle / Reset
steps.instructions: Turn the unit off and restart to clear minor faults.

CCR.Complaint: i-STAT 1 analyzer not charging and shutting down quickly during use.
CCR.Cause: Worn battery with reduced capacity and poor charging contact.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned contacts, tested with known-good dock, and removed unit from service for battery replacement.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service vs out of service)

title: Abbott i-STAT 1 Blood Gas Analyzer - Display or Keypad Failure
description: Unresponsive keypad, blank or dim screen, or incorrect display behavior caused by battery, contamination, or external damage.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Do not troubleshoot while the analyzer is needed for active patient testing. Move testing to another analyzer.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the analyzer and attempt normal operation. Observe: Blank or dim screen, Partial display or distorted text, Keypad buttons not responding or delayed response

steps.title: Check Battery Condition (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Low or unstable battery power can cause display and keypad malfunctions. Remove and reseat the battery. Replace with a known-good, fully charged battery

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Examine the screen and keypad for visible issues. Check for cracks, impact damage, or worn keypad buttons. Look for signs of fluid intrusion

steps.title: Check for Contamination (Very Common in POC Devices)
steps.instructions: Residue from gloves, fluids, or cleaning agents can affect keypad responsiveness. Inspect keypad for sticky or unresponsive buttons. Clean surface using approved disinfectant wipes (per facility policy). Allow device to fully dry before retesting

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset
steps.instructions: Resetting the device can clear temporary faults. Power off the analyzer. Remove battery for 30–60 seconds. Reinstall battery and power back on

steps.title: Check Operating Environment
steps.instructions: Environmental factors can impact display visibility and keypad performance. Verify device is within normal temperature range. Avoid direct bright light affecting screen visibility

CCR.Complaint: Analyzer display blank and keypad unresponsive during blood gas testing.
CCR.Cause: Battery voltage low causing improper display and input function.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery with known-good unit; device returned to normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Signs of contamination or fluid exposure
